findings.description: Session tokens are generated with random.choice (Mersenne Twister, a non-cryptographic PRNG), making auth tokens predictable once the recoverable PRNG state is observed — direct session-hijacking/auth-bypass; use the secrets module (CWE-330/CWE-338, OWASP A07).

touch() renews expiry without checking expires_at or purging, so an already-expired session token can be resurrected indefinitely, defeating the TTL/idle-timeout control (CWE-613 Insufficient Session Expiration).

_sessions grows unbounded — create() never purges and there is no size cap, while _purge_expired only fires lazily on get()/active_count(), enabling memory-exhaustion DoS via repeated session minting (CWE-770/CWE-400); perf critic owns the throughput angle.

Token validation uses dict.get (not constant-time compare_digest), a theoretical timing side-channel (CWE-208); practical leakage for a 32-char token is negligible vs network jitter — borderline, likely dismissible.
